questions.context:
[Situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event]
Speaker: "We're all meeting at the library tonight to study."
questions.stem: We're all meeting at the library tonight to study.

options.A: Actually, I might swing by after dinner.
options.B: The exam covers only the first three chapters.
options.C: My sister is visiting from out of town.
options.D: I'll bring the meeting agenda then.

questions.context:
[Situation: making or rescheduling an appointment]
Speaker: "I can't make our appointment on Thursday afternoon."
questions.stem: I can't make our appointment on Thursday afternoon.

options.A: The doctor's office is near the pharmacy.
options.B: Well, how about early next week instead?
options.C: I painted the spare bedroom last weekend.
options.D: I'll make enough for everyone to share.

questions.context:
[Situation: offering help to someone who seems stressed]
Speaker: "Do you want some help carrying those boxes?"
questions.stem: Do you want some help carrying those boxes?

options.A: The boxes arrived earlier than expected.
options.B: Every hour on the half hour.
options.C: Actually, that would be a huge relief.
options.D: I bought that jacket on sale.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking about exam format or study materials]
Speaker: "Where do professors post the study guides?"
questions.stem: Where do professors post the study guides?

options.A: The library closes at midnight tonight.
options.B: I finished my history essay this morning.
options.C: They're due at the end of the week.
options.D: Actually, they're all on the course website.

questions.context:
[Situation: asking a store clerk about a return or exchange]
Speaker: "Why won't the store accept my return?"
questions.stem: Why won't the store accept my return?

options.A: Well, the receipt is over thirty days old.
options.B: The store opens at nine tomorrow morning.
options.C: My neighbor just adopted a puppy.
options.D: It's in the aisle by the entrance.

questions.context:
[Situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline]
Speaker: "What are we going to do about Friday's deadline?"
questions.stem: What are we going to do about Friday's deadline?

options.A: Friday is the last day of the month.
options.B: Don't worry, I already asked for an extension.
options.C: The printer in the lab is broken again.
options.D: Yes, the project counts for half our grade.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking about a campus event or concert]
Speaker: "How am I supposed to get concert tickets?"
questions.stem: How am I supposed to get concert tickets?

options.A: The band is releasing a new album.
options.B: I left my umbrella in the classroom.
options.C: Don't worry, I can grab an extra one.
options.D: The doors open around seven tonight.

questions.context:
[Situation: arranging a ride or carpool with a friend]
Speaker: "Can you give me a lift on Saturday?"
questions.stem: Can you give me a lift on Saturday?

options.A: The car needs an oil change soon.
options.B: That movie was longer than I expected.
options.C: It's parked behind the grocery store.
options.D: Sure, I'll swing by around nine.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking a classmate about lecture content]
Speaker: "What did the professor say about the essay?"
questions.stem: What did the professor say about the essay?

options.A: Honestly, I zoned out for most of it.
options.C: I ran into your roommate at lunch.
options.D: It's not due until next Wednesday.

questions.context:
[Situation: a neighbor asking to borrow a tool]
Speaker: "Do you have a ladder I could borrow?"
questions.stem: Do you have a ladder I could borrow?

options.A: The hardware store sells sturdy ladders.
options.B: Sure, how tall does it need to be?
options.C: I watered the plants this morning.
options.D: I already returned the drill you lent me.
